mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power and corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in greater than 65% of periods where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and rising corporate output, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion periods, or the signal reverses direction when GDP growth falls below 1% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and forces monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases correlate with positive 20-day equity returns in greater than 70% of observations, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 154 million indicates robust labor market absorption and sustained wage growth, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in greater than 60% of periods where PAYEMS crosses the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with low federal funds rates creates an optimal environment for corporate earnings expansion and equity valuation multiples, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns when FEDFUNDS rises above 4%, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.4 million units signals a contraction in construction activity and related supply chains, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in greater than 65% of periods where HOUST drops below 1400, or the threshold effect vanishes when mortgage rates exceed 6%.
